typical_use: fever, mild-moderate pain
warnings: Hepatotoxic above 4g/day. Avoid in active liver disease.

typical_use: pain, fever, inflammation
warnings: GI bleeding risk. Avoid in active peptic ulcer, advanced renal disease, 3rd trimester pregnancy.

typical_use: mild-moderate pain, dysmenorrhea
warnings: Limit duration to 7 days. NSAID class warnings apply.

typical_use: allergic rhinitis, urticaria, antihistamine; some Long COVID protocols use H1 antihistamines
warnings: May cause sedation. Reduce dose in renal impairment.

typical_use: allergic rhinitis, urticaria; non-sedating H1 antihistamine
warnings: Renal-dose-adjusted. Less sedation than cetirizine.

typical_use: gastric acid suppression; H2 blocker (occasionally paired with H1 in MCAS-Long COVID protocols)
warnings: Reduce dose in renal impairment.

typical_use: allergic rhinitis, urticaria; non-sedating H1 antihistamine
warnings: Generally well tolerated.

typical_use: alcohol/opioid dependence (50mg). Off-label low-dose naltrexone (LDN, 1.5-4.5mg) under investigation for autoimmune conditions and Long COVID — compounding required.
price_idr_range: 180k-350k per month at LDN dose, varies by compounding pharmacy
warnings: LDN dose requires physician supervision and a compounding pharmacy. Off-label in Indonesia. Contraindicated with opioid use.

typical_use: hypertension, anxiety, POTS (off-label tachycardia control)
warnings: Contraindicated in asthma, severe bradycardia, decompensated heart failure. Off-label for POTS.

typical_use: chronic stable angina, heart failure; off-label POTS tachycardia control
warnings: Contraindicated in atrial fibrillation. Off-label for POTS.

typical_use: orthostatic hypotension, neurogenic OH; sometimes used in POTS with hypotensive phenotype
license_status: limited availability in Indonesia; may require import authorization
price_idr_range: highly variable; verify with compounding apotek
warnings: Supine hypertension risk. Do not dose within 4 hours of bedtime.

typical_use: adrenal insufficiency, orthostatic hypotension/POTS volume expansion
warnings: Mineralocorticoid: hypertension, hypokalemia, edema. Monitor electrolytes.

typical_use: sleep onset difficulty, circadian rhythm disruption
warnings: Daytime drowsiness possible. Avoid driving until response known.

typical_use: depression, generalized anxiety disorder
warnings: SSRI: first 2 weeks may worsen anxiety. Black box warning for suicidality in young adults. Do not stop abruptly.

typical_use: bacterial infections; beta-lactam antibiotic
warnings: Penicillin allergy contraindication. Complete full course.

typical_use: bacterial respiratory tract infections; macrolide antibiotic
warnings: QT prolongation risk. Avoid with other QT-prolonging drugs.

typical_use: hypertension, chronic stable angina
warnings: Peripheral edema common. Reduce dose in hepatic impairment.

typical_use: dyslipidemia, cardiovascular risk reduction
warnings: Myopathy risk; report unexplained muscle pain. Liver enzymes monitoring.

typical_use: type 2 diabetes; insulin resistance
warnings: Contraindicated in advanced renal impairment (eGFR <30). Hold around contrast imaging. Vitamin B12 deficiency on long-term use.

typical_use: bronchospasm, asthma, COPD
warnings: Tachycardia, tremor. Excessive use may worsen control.

typical_use: asthma, allergic rhinitis (inhaled corticosteroid)
warnings: Oral candidiasis risk; rinse mouth after use.

rx_class: OTC for low strengths, Rx for higher
typical_use: GERD, peptic ulcer, dyspepsia
warnings: Long-term use: vitamin B12 deficiency, magnesium deficiency, fracture risk.

typical_use: vitamin D deficiency; immune support
warnings: Hypercalcemia risk at very high chronic doses. Verify dosing with clinician for therapeutic levels.

typical_use: magnesium repletion; better-absorbed form than oxide
warnings: Diarrhea at high doses (less than oxide). Reduce dose in renal impairment.

typical_use: dehydration, POTS volume expansion, gastrointestinal fluid losses
warnings: For POTS use, higher sodium content may be needed; discuss with clinician.

typical_use: venous insufficiency, orthostatic intolerance/POTS lower-body pooling reduction
warnings: Fitting matters. Class II (20-30 mmHg) often used for POTS; verify with clinician.

typical_use: secondary cardiovascular prevention; some Long COVID microclot protocols (off-label)
warnings: Bleeding risk. Avoid with other anticoagulants. Off-label for Long COVID microclots.

typical_use: herpes simplex, varicella zoster; off-label suppressive use in ME/CFS subgroups with herpesvirus reactivation
warnings: Renal-dose-adjusted. Maintain hydration. Off-label antiviral suppression in ME/CFS lacks definitive trial evidence.

typical_use: herpes simplex, varicella zoster; off-label suppressive in ME/CFS with EBV/HHV-6 reactivation
references: Lerner et al ME/CFS antiviral studies
warnings: Renal-dose-adjusted. Rare TTP/HUS in immunocompromised. Off-label ME/CFS use experimental — physician supervision required.

typical_use: hypertension; off-label POTS hyperadrenergic phenotype, ADHD, opioid withdrawal
warnings: Rebound hypertension on abrupt discontinuation. Sedation, dry mouth, orthostatic hypotension. Off-label for POTS hyperadrenergic subtype only.

typical_use: myasthenia gravis; off-label POTS (cholinesterase inhibitor improves orthostatic tolerance)
warnings: Cholinergic side effects: cramps, diarrhea, hypersalivation. Contraindicated in mechanical GI/urinary obstruction.

typical_use: neurogenic orthostatic hypotension; investigational POTS hypotensive phenotype
license_status: not BPOM-registered; personal import authorization required
price_idr_range: verify with hospital pharmacy; very expensive
warnings: Supine hypertension; do not dose within 5 hours of bedtime. Limited Indonesian availability.

typical_use: mast cell stabilizer; allergic conjunctivitis, asthma prophylaxis; off-label MCAS
warnings: Sedation, weight gain. Off-label MCAS use requires specialist supervision.

typical_use: mast cell stabilizer; allergic rhinitis, asthma prophylaxis, MCAS GI symptoms (oral form)
license_status: limited availability in Indonesia; oral form often compounded
warnings: Poor oral bioavailability — taken 15-30 min before meals for GI MCAS. Not a rescue medication.

typical_use: allergic rhinitis, urticaria; first-generation sedating H1 antihistamine; sometimes used in MCAS protocols
warnings: Significant sedation, anticholinergic effects. Avoid in elderly (Beers criteria), prostatic hyperplasia, narrow-angle glaucoma.

typical_use: antiplatelet for secondary cardiovascular prevention, post-stent; off-label dual antiplatelet protocols in Long COVID microclot research
references: Pretorius et al Long COVID microclot studies
warnings: Bleeding risk, additive with aspirin. CYP2C19 poor metabolizers have reduced efficacy. Off-label for Long COVID — no large RCT evidence.

typical_use: fibrinolytic supplement from fermented soy; investigational in Long COVID microclot protocols
references: Long COVID microclot research (Pretorius et al)
warnings: Theoretical bleeding risk, especially with antiplatelets/anticoagulants. No definitive RCT evidence for Long COVID. Soy allergy contraindication.

typical_use: fibrinolytic enzyme from earthworms; investigational in Long COVID microclot protocols
price_idr_range: verify with import pharmacy; typically 1jt+ per bottle
warnings: Theoretical bleeding risk with antiplatelets/anticoagulants. Limited safety data. No definitive RCT evidence for Long COVID.

typical_use: depression, OCD, panic disorder, PTSD, social anxiety
warnings: SSRI: initial anxiety/insomnia, GI upset, sexual dysfunction. Black box for suicidality in young adults. Do not stop abruptly. Serotonin syndrome risk with other serotonergic agents.

typical_use: depression, OCD, bulimia, premenstrual dysphoric disorder
warnings: SSRI with long half-life. Initial anxiety, sexual dysfunction. Black box for suicidality in young adults. Multiple CYP2D6/3A4 interactions.

typical_use: depression, generalized anxiety, diabetic neuropathy, fibromyalgia, chronic musculoskeletal pain
warnings: SNRI: blood pressure elevation, hepatotoxicity, withdrawal syndrome on abrupt stop. Avoid in uncontrolled narrow-angle glaucoma.

typical_use: depression, especially with insomnia or weight loss; off-label appetite stimulant
warnings: Sedation (paradoxically less at higher doses), significant weight gain, rare agranulocytosis. Caution in hepatic impairment.

typical_use: depression; off-label insomnia (low dose)
warnings: Orthostatic hypotension, sedation, rare priapism (urgent care required). QT prolongation at higher doses.

typical_use: panic disorder, short-term anxiety
warnings: HIGH abuse and dependence potential. Withdrawal can be severe (seizures). Schedule IV psikotropika — strict prescription requirements in Indonesia. Avoid in respiratory depression, sleep apnea. Do not combine with opioids.

typical_use: anxiety, alcohol withdrawal, status epilepticus, muscle spasm
warnings: HIGH abuse and dependence potential. Long half-life — accumulation in elderly. Schedule IV psikotropika. Avoid in respiratory depression, severe hepatic impairment. Do not combine with opioids.

typical_use: dyslipidemia, cardiovascular risk reduction
warnings: Myopathy/rhabdomyolysis risk, especially at higher doses or with CYP3A4 inhibitors (avoid grapefruit). Liver enzyme monitoring. Avoid in active liver disease.

typical_use: hypertension, heart failure, post-MI; ACE inhibitor
warnings: Cough (10-15%), hyperkalemia, angioedema (rare but life-threatening). Contraindicated in pregnancy, bilateral renal artery stenosis. Monitor renal function and potassium.

typical_use: hypertension, heart failure; angiotensin receptor blocker (ARB)
warnings: Hyperkalemia, renal impairment. Contraindicated in pregnancy. Lower cough rate than ACE inhibitors. Past NDMA recalls — verify supplier batch.

typical_use: edema in heart failure, hepatic cirrhosis, renal disease; hypertension; loop diuretic
warnings: Hypokalemia, hyponatremia, dehydration, ototoxicity at high IV doses. Generally avoid in POTS (volume depletion worsens orthostatic intolerance).

typical_use: heart failure, resistant hypertension, primary hyperaldosteronism, ascites; off-label hyperandrogenism, acne
warnings: Hyperkalemia (monitor potassium and renal function). Gynecomastia, menstrual irregularities. Avoid in significant renal impairment. Not first-line for POTS — usually contraindicated as volume depletion worsens orthostatic symptoms.

typical_use: hypothyroidism, thyroid hormone replacement, TSH suppression post-thyroidectomy
warnings: Narrow therapeutic index. Take on empty stomach, 30-60 min before food/coffee/calcium/iron. Over-replacement risks atrial fibrillation, osteoporosis. Adjust dose by TSH.

typical_use: hypothyroidism (selected cases of poor T4-to-T3 conversion); occasionally used in refractory depression augmentation
license_status: limited availability in Indonesia; specialist import
warnings: Short half-life — multiple daily doses. Higher risk of overshoot (palpitations, anxiety, bone loss) than T4. Specialist supervision strongly recommended.

typical_use: B12 deficiency, megaloblastic anemia, peripheral neuropathy; supplementation in PPI/metformin long-term users and vegans
references: NIH ODS vitamin B12 fact sheet
warnings: Generally well tolerated. Confirm deficiency with serum B12 (and ideally MMA/homocysteine) before chronic high-dose supplementation. Injectable form for malabsorption.

typical_use: iron deficiency anemia; better-tolerated form than ferrous sulfate
warnings: GI side effects less than sulfate but still possible. Verify ferritin before chronic supplementation — iron overload risk. Avoid in hemochromatosis.

typical_use: mitochondrial support; investigational in ME/CFS, fibromyalgia, statin-induced myopathy
warnings: Generally well tolerated. May reduce warfarin efficacy (structural similarity to vitamin K). Limited but suggestive evidence for ME/CFS fatigue.

typical_use: mucolytic for respiratory conditions; paracetamol overdose antidote; investigational antioxidant in Long COVID, mental health
warnings: GI upset, rare bronchospasm in asthma (inhaled). Sulfur smell. Limited RCT evidence for Long COVID despite enthusiasm.

typical_use: bioflavonoid; investigational mast cell stabilizer (MCAS adjunct), antioxidant
warnings: Generally well tolerated. Theoretical CYP3A4 interactions at high doses. Limited RCT evidence for MCAS — adjunct only.

typical_use: anti-inflammatory supplement; investigational in chronic inflammation, joint pain, Long COVID
warnings: Bioavailability poor without piperine or lipid formulation. Theoretical bleeding risk with anticoagulants. Rare cholestatic hepatitis reports.

typical_use: iron-binding glycoprotein from milk; investigational gut microbiome support and immune modulation in Long COVID research
warnings: Generally well tolerated. Milk protein source — contraindicated in severe dairy allergy. Limited RCT evidence in Long COVID.

typical_use: gut microbiome support; specific strains studied for IBS, antibiotic-associated diarrhea, post-infectious dysbiosis (incl. Long COVID research)
warnings: Strain specificity matters — not all probiotics equivalent. Caution in severe immunocompromise, central venous catheters (rare bacteremia).

typical_use: parasitic infections — scabies, strongyloidiasis, onchocerciasis, head lice (topical). Antiparasitic.
license_status: BPOM-registered for parasitic infections (scabies, strongyloidiasis). NOT recommended for COVID-19 per WHO and BPOM — large clinical trials have shown no benefit. Listing here only for accurate Indonesian formulary mapping.
price_idr_range: 50k-300k per strip oral; 80k-200k per cream topical
warnings: For parasitic infections only. WHO and BPOM do not recommend for COVID-19; multiple large RCTs (TOGETHER, ACTIV-6) showed no benefit. Off-label COVID dosing risks neurotoxicity, hepatotoxicity. Contraindicated with high CNS-penetration concerns in some patient groups.

typical_use: asthma maintenance and reliever therapy (MART/SMART), COPD; combined inhaled corticosteroid + long-acting beta-2 agonist
warnings: Oral candidiasis — rinse mouth after use. Tachycardia, tremor from formoterol. LABA monotherapy in asthma is contraindicated; only use as combined ICS-LABA. Monitor for paradoxical bronchospasm.
